title: bromus sterilis var. tectorum
intro: Bromus sterilis var. tectorum, also known as cheat grass, is a type of perennial grass that belongs to the family Poaceae and is native to Europe and Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, due to its ability to soothe and calm the body. Traditionally, Bromus sterilis var. tectorum is prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to harness its medicinal benefits and is also used in herbal remedies to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation.
